name: Jamil Nasser
abstract: Jamil Nasser (born George Joyner, June 21, 1932 – February 13, 2010) and also credited on some of Ahmad Jamal's recordings as Jamil Sulieman, was an American jazz musician. He played double bass, electric bass, and tuba.

concerts.label: Lecture and Concert by Ahmad Jamal Trio and Earl Coleman Ensemble
Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: My Favorite Things: A Tribute to Richard Rodgers
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works.t: Babes in Arms: My Funny Valentine
Pal Joey: Bewitched, Bothered and Bewildered
Pal Joey: I Could Write a Book
The Sound of Music: My Favorite Things

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: And I went up to play and it was with Jon Hendricks, the singer, who just happened to be there. And there was a great bass player, Jamil Nasser and Harold Mabern, a great pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: And then, Jamil Nasser, Vishnu Wood and Jimmy Owens came to them one day and said, “You know, this is great that you want to do all these things. But you have musicians here who are having serious health issues, they can’t pay their rent, and there’s no one taking care of them.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: And I’m like, I’m frozen. I don’t know what that is. And he turned around and he looked at me... Jamil Nasser is playing and the drummer is playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: Vishnu Wood and Jamil Nasser, and Jimmy Owens came to them and said, “There’s all these musicians having terrible issues. We need to do something.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 22, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Lou Takes Off, which is a sextet with Curtis Fuller, Donald Byrd and Sonny Clark... [INTERVIEWEE] And Jamil Nasser.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
